meta.homeTitle: El Rayga Camp — Dahab, Sinai · Huts, rooms & hostel beds by the Red Sea
meta.homeDesc: El Rayga Camp (كامب الرايجه) on the Masbat waterfront in Dahab. Bamboo huts, private rooms and hostel beds from EGP 350 — rated 4.6★ on Google by 492 travellers. Book direct.
meta.stayTitle: Stay & Book — huts, rooms & hostel beds
meta.stayDesc: Book El Rayga Camp direct: seafront bamboo huts, garden huts, private AC rooms, studios and hostel dorm beds from EGP 350 a night in Masbat, Dahab.
meta.reviewsDesc: Every El Rayga Camp review from around the internet in one place: 4.6★ from 492 Google reviews, 8.0 on Booking.com, Tripadvisor and Facebook.
meta.expDesc: Days at El Rayga Camp: dive the Blue Hole, freedive the reef, sunrise yoga, camel safaris, fire and film nights, snorkelling off the Masbat shore.
meta.aboutTitle: About — the camp, the courtyard, the people
meta.aboutDesc: The story of El Rayga Camp: an old-school palm-wood camp on Peace Road, Masbat, Dahab — its courtyard, its kitchen, and the people who run it.
meta.contactTitle: Contact & Find Us — Peace Rd, Masbat, Dahab
meta.contactDesc: Find El Rayga Camp: Peace Rd, Masbat, Dahab, South Sinai 46617. Call or WhatsApp +20 103 006 9058. Plus code FGX8+7C.

hero.sub: Bamboo huts, breezy rooms and honest hostel beds on the Masbat waterfront — two barefoot minutes from the lagoon, one Bedouin tea from your new favourite people.

home.introLead: “Rayga” is Dahab slang for that state of total, salty, sun-warmed calm. We built a camp around it: palm-wood huts and simple rooms wrapped around a green courtyard on Peace Road, steps from the Masbat promenade. No lobby music. No dress code. Just the reef, the mountains across the gulf, and tea that never runs out.
home.whereNote: Plus code FGX8+7C. Alaska Camp and the Volunteers' house are neighbours; the Ugarit Syria kitchen is on-site and open until 2 AM.
home.mapAlt: Illustrated map of the Dahab lagoon and the camp's spot on the Masbat waterfront

stay.lead: Every stay books direct with real availability — pick dates on the room page, pay online with Paymob or on arrival. Huts share the (twice-daily cleaned) bathrooms; rooms and studios have their own.
stay.marquee1: Free cancellation up to 48h on direct bookings

room.noChargeNote: Pay online with Paymob or at the desk on arrival — free cancellation up to 48 h.
room.pickDatesFirst: Pick your check-in and check-out dates first.
room.rangeCrossesFull: Those dates cross a sold-out night — pick another range.
room.cancellation: Free cancellation up to 48 h before check-in on direct bookings · Check-in {in} · Check-out {out}

checkout.title: Complete your
checkout.notesPlaceholder: Arrival time, dietary stuff, airport pickup, celebrating something…
checkout.method_arrival_desc: Cash (EGP) or card at the desk. Free cancellation up to 48 h before check-in.
checkout.method_paymob_card_desc: Visa / Mastercard / Meeza via Paymob's secure checkout. Instant confirmation.
checkout.method_paymob_wallet_desc: Vodafone Cash, Orange Money, Etisalat Cash and more, via Paymob.
checkout.footnote: Your dates are held while you complete this page. Online payments are processed by Paymob; we never see your card.
checkout.emptyLead: Pick a hut, room or bed and choose your dates first.

confirmed.lead_paid: Paid and locked in — your bed is officially yours. A WhatsApp message to the desk helps us prep your arrival.
confirmed.lead_confirmed: Your booking is confirmed — you pay at the desk when you arrive. Send it to WhatsApp so the night shift knows to expect you.
confirmed.lead_pending: Your booking is in and awaiting payment confirmation. If you closed the payment window, contact the desk on WhatsApp.
confirmed.bring: Bring: sunscreen, a book you're willing to trade, and no plans whatsoever.

reviews.lead: Pulled from Google Maps, Booking.com, Tripadvisor and Facebook — unedited, including the ones that sting. Camps are honest places; so is this page.
reviews.note: Quotes with a platform badge are published guest reviews on that platform; cards marked “theme summary” condense recurring feedback across many reviews. Scores checked July 2026.
reviews.marquee2: “My 3rd stay — I fall in love more each time”

experiences.lead: Everything below is bookable at the front desk — ask for Dawod. Prices are per person, gear included unless it says otherwise, and nothing starts before coffee.
experiences.chipTea: Bedouin tea at the fire pit
experiences.chipGames: Board games & a tricked-out chess set

dive.title: Dive the Blue Hole & Lighthouse
dive.desc: Two guided dives with a licensed centre 200 m up the promenade. All levels; try-dives daily at 9.

freedive.title: Freediving intro course
freedive.desc: One breath, twelve metres, a very quiet brain. Half-day intro with certified instructors on the house reef.

snorkel.title: Snorkel safari — Three Pools & Ras Abu Galum
snorkel.desc: Boat-free day trip along the coast, lunch in a Bedouin hut on the sand. Gear and pickup included.

yoga.title: Sunrise yoga on the terrace
yoga.desc: 60 minutes, all levels, mats provided. The sun does the savasana lighting for free.

safari.title: Camel & jeep desert safari
safari.desc: Laguna camel ride at sunset or Wadi Gnai by jeep — Bedouin dinner, star talk, zero light pollution.

fire.title: Fire & film night
fire.desc: Every Thursday: a screen in the courtyard, tea on the coals, and whatever the camp votes to watch.

about.lead: Ask anyone on the promenade what الرايجه means and they'll shrug, smile, and point at the sea. It's Dahab slang for that unhurried, salt-cured calm you get after a few days here. We named the camp after it because that's the whole product.
about.mediaAlt: The camp courtyard at dusk, huts and string lights
about.placeBody: El Rayga sits in Masbat, the old heart of Dahab, one row back from the water. Palm-wood huts and simple rooms wrap around a green inner courtyard — bougainvillea, hammocks, a fire pit, and the Ugarit Syria kitchen serving until 2 AM. Alaska Camp and the Dahab Volunteers house are our neighbours; the lagoon is a two-minute barefoot walk.
about.ideaBody: Before the hotels, Dahab was camps: palm walls, mattresses, tea, and the reef. Most of them are gone or gentrified. We keep the format and fix the parts that deserved fixing — real mattresses, cold AC where it counts, bathrooms cleaned twice a day, Wi-Fi that reaches the hammocks. Simple isn't the same as sloppy.
about.peopleBody: Guests keep writing it, so we'll say it: Dawod at the front desk is the spirit of the camp — a recent Google review called him exactly that. The desk runs 24 hours, books your dives and safaris, and remembers how you take your tea by day two. Solo travellers arrive alone and leave with a group photo.
about.honestyTitle: We're a camp, not a resort
about.honestyBody: Huts have shared bathrooms. The desert has mosquitoes at dusk. Music from the promenade drifts in on Fridays. When we get a rough review — and we have — we read it at the desk and fix what's fixable. The rest is camp life, and it's the point. Read every review, good and bad, on the reviews page.

contact.mapAlt: Illustrated view over the Dahab lagoon at dawn
contact.formLead: WhatsApp is fastest — the desk answers around the clock. This form drafts the message for you and opens WhatsApp; nothing is sent anywhere else.
contact.name: Name
contact.messagePlaceholder: Beds? Huts? Airport pickup? Ask anything.
contact.directions: From Sharm el-Sheikh airport: 1 h 15 by taxi or the go-bus to Dahab station, then a 5-minute tuk-tuk to Masbat. Tell the driver “Alaska Camp” — we're the green gate next door with the lanterns. Airport pickup can be arranged at the desk.

footer.cta: Come live the Rayga. Beds from EGP 350 a night, two minutes from the lagoon.

seafront-bamboo-hut.name: Seafront Bamboo Hut
seafront-bamboo-hut.tagline: Fall asleep to the Gulf of Aqaba, wake up two steps from it.
seafront-bamboo-hut.desc: Our signature bamboo hut on the front row of the camp. Woven palm walls, a proper mattress with fresh linen, and a private patio that looks straight across the lagoon to the Saudi mountains. Simple on purpose — this is the classic Dahab camp experience the Masbat strip was built on.

garden-hut.name: Garden Hut
garden-hut.tagline: A quiet hut wrapped in bougainvillea, off the courtyard.
garden-hut.desc: Tucked into the green inner courtyard, away from the promenade. Twin beds, woven bamboo, and the sound of the garden instead of the street. Shared bathrooms are a short barefoot walk away. The pick for light sleepers who still want the camp life.

bedouin-hut.name: Bedouin Hut — Single
bedouin-hut.tagline: One bed, one lantern, one million stars.
bedouin-hut.desc: The solo traveller's hut. A single mattress on a raised palm-wood base, Bedouin rugs, a brass lantern, and just enough room for a backpack and a good book. It's the cheapest private space in the camp and the most asked-for after sunset tea at the fire pit.

comfy-studio.name: Comfy Studio
comfy-studio.tagline: Your own kitchen, your own keys, camp on your doorstep.
comfy-studio.desc: A proper studio for slow travellers and remote workers. Air-conditioning, a fully equipped kitchenette with fridge and microwave, a comfortable double bed and a desk that actually works for working. Stay a week, stay a season — long-stay rates on request.

private-double.name: Private Double Room
private-double.tagline: Camp soul, hotel sleep. AC, ensuite, done.
private-double.desc: A solid, spotless private room off the courtyard with air-conditioning and its own bathroom. Thick walls, blackout curtains and a real mattress — for couples and anyone who loves the camp vibe but negotiates hard on sleep quality.

family-lodge.name: Family Lodge
family-lodge.tagline: Four beds, one courtyard, zero screens required.
family-lodge.desc: The biggest room in the camp: a double bed plus two singles, air-conditioning, ensuite bathroom and a shaded seating nook outside the door. Kids get the garden, the rabbits and the lagoon; you get Bedouin tea and an actual holiday.

mixed-dorm.name: Mixed Dorm — 5-Bed AC
mixed-dorm.tagline: The backpacker classic. Cold AC, warm people.
mixed-dorm.desc: Five proper single beds — no squeaky bunk towers — in an air-conditioned shared room with personal lockers, reading lights and sockets by every bed. Shared bathrooms across the courtyard. Half of Dahab's best friendships started in this room.

female-dorm.name: Female Dorm — 4-Bed AC
female-dorm.tagline: Women-only, four beds, extra mirrors, same sea.
female-dorm.desc: A women-only air-conditioned dorm with four single beds, curtained for privacy, personal lockers and a large mirror corner. Steps from the shared kitchen and the quiet end of the courtyard. Solo female travellers rate this — and Dahab — very highly.
